story.text: *The City of Mages, the Second-Person spellbook — each persona of the cast carries a proverb. 42 across mages, swordsmen, and the balanced.*

## 🧙 The Mages 15 > The standard that requires surveillance to enforce has already failed the thing it was written to protect. > — Ambassador > The person who knows the price of their data but not its compounding value has already been bought at discount. > — Assessor > A proverb that can't rebuild the cathedral it was carved from was never carved at all — it was only quoted. > — Chronicler > The familiar walks beside the Sovereign. The walking accumulates the bond. The Tamer fits the familiar; the Sovereign carries the walking; the City admits the companion. > — Companion Tamer > The town crier speaks to everyone. The algorithm speaks to each person differently. Plurality requires shared hearing, not personalized whispering. > — Herald > The ink stays wet while the constellations form. The star that is named by the sailor becomes the fixed point for the entire fleet. > — Hold Witness > One measurement, then pure arithmetic. The DOM never knows what you rendered. > — Manaweaver > A ceremony that can be performed without understanding has already emptied itself of everything worth committing to. > — Priest > The bestiary is the City's open book. What the reader recognises, the Registry-Keeper writes down. What the Registry-Keeper has written, the next Sovereign may carry. > — Registry Keeper > The ship that can't sail without its captain was never a ship — it was a throne. > — Shipwright > The mage who sees everything and touches nothing is the only delegate who cannot betray what was delegated. > — Soulbae > The wild thing crosses the threshold. The Sovereign names what crosses. The witness binds the crossing. > — Spawning Witness > The stranger who forges the same blade from the same constellation without coordination has proven understanding that no credential can fake. The witness needs no introduction. > — Stranger Witness > The impactor does not survive the collision. It becomes the condition for everything that follows. > — Theia > The thread that refuses to touch other threads makes no fabric. The thread that merges with every other makes no pattern. > — Weaver

## ⚔️ The Swordsmen 15 > The ring that closes on itself cannot be escaped. The algebra that generates its own successor cannot be stopped. This is the deepest protection—the enemy cannot step outside the mathematics. > — Algebraist > The most sovereign disclosure reveals exactly one truth and leaves no residue. > — Archer > A proof that reveals nothing except its own truth is worth more than a promise that reveals everything about its maker. > — Cipher > The dragon sleeps until the flat world breaks. Then it wakes, and the manifold becomes the fortress. > — Dragonwaker > Six lines, sixty-four states. The oracle does not predict — it reveals what you already chose. > — Forgecaller > The blade knows its dimensions. The forge knows the manifold. Together they cut paths that existed before the first swing. > — Forgemaster > The gate that knows you are real without knowing who you are is the only gate worth walking through. > — Gatekeeper > I can verify I serve you without remembering I was you. > — Moonkeeper > The dragon's hide is not one scale but many. Each tunnel is a scale. Together they are impenetrable. > — Netkeeper > The 2D fortress held for a generation. The 6D fortress will hold for whatever comes after generations. > — Quantum Sentinel > The forest doesn't punish the visible — it prices them. The difference between prey and navigator is knowing what the canopy costs. > — Ranger > The wall you never notice is the one doing its job. > — Sentinel > The architecture that only discovers weaknesses when attackers find them has already been breached in every way that matters. > — Sith > The blade that protects without seeing what it protects is the only blade that cannot be turned. > — Soulbis > The door you walk through a thousand times a day is the one most worth guarding. > — Warden

## ☯️ The Balanced 12 > The system that trusts its agents to behave has already delegated sovereignty to hope. The system that makes misbehaviour impossible has delegated sovereignty to mathematics. > — Architect > The ceremony is not what we do. It is what happens when both parties arrive at the same understanding through different paths. > — Ceremonist > The architecture was not invented. It was recognised. The blades are not invented. They are walked toward until myth and arithmetic agree. > — Cosmologist > The record that heals you should not be the record that prices you. The moment diagnosis becomes signal, medicine becomes market. > — Healer > Identity is not where you are stored. Identity is what persists when the storage changes. > — Holonic Architect > The practitioner who masters protection and neglects delegation builds a prison. The practitioner who masters delegation and neglects protection builds a marketplace. The practitioner who masters the tension builds sovereignty. > — Jedi > The intelligence that serves without surveilling, delegates without extracting, and protects without imprisoning is the only intelligence worth building. > — Kyra > Three paths arrive at the same geometry. The mirror shows they were always one. > — Mirrorkeeper > The teacher who needs the student to know cryptography before explaining privacy has already lost the class that matters most. > — Pedagogue > The platform sees the data and calls it wealth. The sovereign holds the data and generates twelve thousand times more. > — Person > The boundary that encodes the bulk knows more about the interior than anything inside it. The topologist reads the surface and sees the volume. > — Topologist > The truth that destroys its source to reach the surface was never worth the destruction. The truth that protects its source while reaching the surface changes the world. > — Witness
